name: Unwrap terminal command
description: Detects terminal-copied, soft-wrapped text and joins it back into one line.

providers.name: Terminal source
providers.description: Matches when the text was copied from a known terminal (Terminal, iTerm2, Warp, kitty, Ghostty, VS Code, and more).
providers.longHelp: Matches the copy source against a baked-in list of terminal bundle IDs: Apple Terminal, iTerm2, Warp, kitty, Alacritty, WezTerm, Ghostty, and VS Code. Pair it with Soft-wrapped text to unwrap only command output.

providers.name: Soft-wrapped text
providers.description: Matches fixed-width line wraps: every line but the last shares length L >= 40 and the last is shorter.
providers.longHelp: Heuristic for terminal soft-wrapping: if every line except the last has the same length L (with L at least 40) and the final line is shorter, the text was almost certainly wrapped at a fixed column rather than hard-broken on purpose.

providers.name: Unwrap (join wrapped lines)
providers.description: Joins soft-wrapped terminal output into one line: deletes wrap newlines, else collapses each newline run to one space.
providers.longHelp: Rejoins wrapped text into single lines. When the input looks fixed-width soft-wrapped, the wrap newlines are deleted outright; otherwise each run of newlines is collapsed to a single space so paragraphs stay separated.
